title: Guzmania strobilantha
taxonomy: Guzmania strobilantha is classified within the family Bromeliaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Guzmania, which consists of tropical epiphytes. The genus Guzmania is further classified within the subfamily Tillandsioideae. The family Bromeliaceae is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Guzmania strobilantha.

intro: Guzmania strobilantha, also known as the Pineapple Guzmania, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Guzmania strobilantha include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments.
